questions.question: What is the primary function of the hormone thyroxine in the human body?
Which of the following is an example of a negative feedback mechanism in the human body?

options.A: The regulation of blood sugar levels by insulin and glucagon
options.B: The regulation of blood pressure by the kidneys
options.C: The regulation of body temperature by sweating

questions.question: What is the role of the hormone adrenaline in the 'fight or flight' response?

options.B: To increase heart rate and blood pressure

questions.question: Which of the following endocrine glands is responsible for the production of the hormone oxytocin?
What is the term for the phenomenon where the presence of one hormone affects the production or function of another hormone?
Which of the following is an example of a tropic hormone?
What is the primary function of the hormone antidiuretic hormone (ADH) in the human body?

options.C: To regulate water balance in the body

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a hormone that plays a role in the regulation of growth and development?
What is the term for the phenomenon where the binding of one hormone to its receptor affects the binding of another hormone to its receptor?
Which of the following endocrine glands is responsible for the production of the hormone prolactin?
What is the primary function of the hormone follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) in the human body?

options.B: To regulate the production of sperm
options.C: To regulate the production of eggs
options.D: To regulate the production of sex hormones

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a hormone that plays a role in the regulation of electrolyte balance in the body?
What is the term for the phenomenon where the presence of one hormone affects the production or function of another hormone, resulting in an increase in the production of the second hormone?
Which of the following endocrine glands is responsible for the production of the hormone parathyroid hormone (PTH)?
What is the primary function of the hormone gastrin in the human body?

options.A: To regulate the production of stomach acid
options.B: To regulate the production of digestive enzymes
options.C: To regulate the movement of food through the digestive system
options.D: To regulate the absorption of nutrients

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a hormone that plays a role in the regulation of blood sugar levels?
What is the term for the phenomenon where the binding of a hormone to its receptor results in a decrease in the production of the hormone?
Which of the following endocrine glands is responsible for the production of the hormone cortisol?
What is the primary function of the hormone somatostatin in the human body?

options.A: To regulate the production of growth hormone
options.B: To regulate the production of thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH)
options.C: To regulate the production of insulin
options.D: To regulate the production of glucagon

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a hormone that plays a role in the regulation of the menstrual cycle?
